response:
Of course! Here is a comprehensive home theater rubric, designed to be adaptable for evaluating a wide range of systems—from a simple living room setup to a dedicated theater room.

The rubric is broken down into **Key Categories**, each with specific **Criteria**, a **Scoring Scale**, and space for **Notes**. You can weight categories differently based on your priorities (e.g., a movie buff might weight "Picture Quality" higher than "Gaming Performance").

---

### **Home Theater Evaluation Rubric**

**System Being Evaluated:** _________________________________________
**Evaluator:** ________________________ **Date:** __________________
**Total Score:** ______ / 100

---

#### **Category 1: Picture Quality (Max 25 Points)**
| Criteria | Excellent (5) | Good (3-4) | Fair (1-2) | Poor (0) | Score | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Resolution & Sharpness** | Native 4K/8K, pixel-perfect, exceptionally sharp details. | 1080p/Good 4K upscaling, generally sharp, minor softness. | Visible softness, lack of fine detail, upscaling artifacts. | Consistently blurry, poor source handling. | __/5 | |
| **Contrast & Black Levels** | Deep, inky blacks, excellent shadow detail, high dynamic range (HDR). | Good blacks, decent shadow detail, acceptable HDR. | Blacks appear grayish (elevated black floor), crushed shadows. | Very poor contrast, no depth, washed-out image. | __/5 | |
| **Color Accuracy & Vibrancy** | Colors are accurate, vibrant, and natural. Excellent HDR color volume. | Colors are pleasing and mostly accurate. Good saturation. | Colors appear slightly off (too cool/warm), muted, or oversaturated. | Colors are unnatural, garish, or severely dull. | __/5 | |
| **Motion Handling** | Smooth, clear motion with no judder or soap-opera effect (unless intended). | Minor motion blur or judder in fast pans, generally watchable. | Noticeable motion issues, distracting blur or stutter. | Severe motion problems, making action unwatchable. | __/5 | |
| **Uniformity & Artifacts** | No banding, dirty screen effect, vignetting, or screen door effect. | Minor, infrequent artifacts only noticeable on test patterns. | Visible uniformity issues or compression artifacts in content. | Severe banding, DSE, or artifacts that distract during normal viewing. | __/5 | |

---

#### **Category 2: Sound Quality (Max 25 Points)**
| Criteria | Excellent (5) | Good (3-4) | Fair (1-2) | Poor (0) | Score | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Clarity & Dialogue** | Dialogue is crystal clear and intelligible at all volumes. | Dialogue is clear most of the time, may require minor volume adjustments. | Dialogue is sometimes muffled or lost in the mix. | Consistently hard to understand speech without subtitles. | __/5 | |
| **Bass Impact & Quality** | Bass is deep, tight, tactile, and room-filling without being boomy. | Good bass presence, can feel some impact, generally well-controlled. | Bass is either weak/lacking or loose/boomy. | Bass is either absent or a distorted, one-note rumble. | __/5 | |
| **Immersion & Soundstage** | Seamless 360° soundscape, precise object placement, great height effects. | Good surround engagement, clear left/right/center separation. | Limited surround effect, sound feels "front-heavy." | No immersion, all sound comes from a single direction. | __/5 | |
| **Dynamic Range & Balance** | Handles whispers to explosions effortlessly. All channels are perfectly balanced. | Good balance, may need to adjust volume between quiet and loud scenes. | Large volume swings needed, some channels overpower others. | Poor balance, constantly adjusting volume, channels mismatched. | __/5 | |
| **Calibration & Room Integration** | Expertly calibrated (e.g., Dirac, Audyssey), sounds cohesive with the room. | Good auto-calibration results, room integration is acceptable. | Noticeable room issues (echoes, nulls), poor calibration. | Severe acoustic problems, sound is disjointed and unpleasant. | __/5 | |

---

#### **Category 3: System Integration & Ease of Use (Max 20 Points)**
| Criteria | Excellent (4) | Good (2-3) | Fair (1) | Poor (0) | Score | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Source Switching & Control** | Single remote/universal control works flawlessly. Fast, seamless input switching. | Most functions controlled easily, occasional input or CEC hiccups. | Requires multiple remotes, switching can be slow or buggy. | Constant frustration with controls and inputs. | __/4 | |
| **User Interface & Menus** | UI is fast, intuitive, logical, and visually appealing. | UI is functional and mostly easy to navigate. | UI is slow, clunky, or confusing to find settings. | UI is painfully slow and counter-intuitive. | __/4 | |
| **Calibration Accessibility** | User-friendly calibration tools with clear guidance (e.g., on-screen mic setup). | Tools are available but may be buried in menus or slightly complex. | Calibration is difficult for the average user, poor instructions. | No meaningful calibration tools available. | __/4 | |
| **Connectivity & Future-Proofing** | Ample, modern ports (HDMI 2.1, eARC), support for latest codecs, easy to upgrade. | Sufficient ports for current needs, may lack some latest specs. | Limited ports, may require switches, already outdated. | Severely limited connectivity, major upgrade needed. | __/4 | |
| **Everyday Reliability** | System turns on/off flawlessly, no glitches, consistently works as expected. | Works reliably most of the time with rare, minor issues. | Occasional glitches require troubleshooting or resets. | Unreliable, frequent problems disrupt viewing. | __/4 | |

---

#### **Category 4: Room & Viewing Experience (Max 15 Points)**
| Criteria | Excellent (3) | Good (2) | Fair (1) | Poor (0) | Score | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Seating Comfort & Layout** | Seating is extremely comfortable with optimal viewing angles for all. | Comfortable for main seat, secondary seats have acceptable views. | Comfort is mediocre, or some seats have poor views. | Uncomfortable seating and/or very poor sightlines. | __/3 | |
| **Light Control & Ambiance** | Perfect light control (blackout curtains/dark walls), ambient lighting options. | Good light control, can achieve a sufficiently dark environment. | Some ambient light interferes with picture quality. | Uncontrolled bright light severely washes out the image. | __/3 | |
| **Acoustic Treatment** | Purposeful absorption/diffusion panels manage reflections and reverb. | Room furnishings (rugs, curtains, furniture) provide decent sound damping. | Room is very "live" (echoey) or "dead," affecting sound quality. | Severe acoustic issues (e.g., empty room, hard surfaces everywhere). | __/3 | |
| **Aesthetic & Cable Management** | Clean, intentional design. Cables are completely hidden and organized. | Looks good, cables are mostly managed and out of sight. | Functional but cluttered look, some visible cable runs. | Messy, distracting cables and poor equipment placement. | __/3 | |
| **Thermal & Noise Management** | Equipment is silent, no fan noise, and the room remains comfortable. | Minor fan noise only during intense scenes, not distracting. | Noticeable fan noise or heat output from equipment. | Loud equipment noise or excessive heat distracts from experience. | __/3 | |

---

#### **Category 5: Value & Overall Enjoyment (Max 15 Points)**
| Criteria | Excellent (5) | Good (3-4) | Fair (1-2) | Poor (0) | Score | Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Performance vs. Cost** | Exceptional performance for the price point; outstanding value. | Good performance for the money; meets expectations. | Performance is acceptable but price feels slightly high. | Performance does not justify the cost; poor value. | __/5 | |
| **"Wow" Factor & Enjoyment** | Consistently delivers a thrilling, cinematic experience. Pure enjoyment. | Frequently enjoyable and impressive for most content. | Provides basic function but rarely feels special or immersive. | The experience is lackluster, boring, or frustrating. | __/5 | |
| **Gaming Performance (if applicable)** | Low input lag, VRR/ALLM support, fast motion, ideal for all game types. | Good gaming performance with minor compromises. | Playable but with noticeable lag or visual compromises. | High lag or poor picture settings make gaming unpleasant. | __/5 | |

---

### **Scoring & Evaluation Guide**

*   **90-100: Reference Grade** –
